NEWS: 'Microsoft’s market cap surpasses $2.5 trillion, solidifying its position as a top tech giant.
Microsoft reports Q3 2023 revenue of $56.5 billion, driven by cloud and productivity software growth.
The company announces a $60 billion stock buyback program, its largest ever.
Microsoft’s market cap surpasses $2.5 trillion, solidifying its position as a top tech giant.
The board approves a 10% dividend increase, marking the 18th consecutive annual raise.
Azure revenue grows 27% year-over-year, reaching $25 billion in Q3.
Morgan Stanley upgrades Microsoft to Overweight with a $400 price target, citing AI leadership.
UBS downgrades Microsoft due to concerns over slowing cloud growth and macroeconomic risks.
Goldman Sachs raises its price target to $420, highlighting Microsoft’s AI and cloud dominance.

The company is added to the Dow Jones Sustainability Index for its ESG efforts.
Microsoft unveils Windows 12, featuring advanced AI integration and a new user interface.
The Surface Pro 10 is announced, powered by Qualcomm’s Snapdragon X Elite chip.
Microsoft delays the launch of its next-gen HoloLens 3 to 2025 due to technical challenges.
The company introduces Copilot Pro, an AI-powered productivity tool for Office 365.
Microsoft’s Xbox Series X refresh includes a redesigned chassis and improved cooling.
The company announces a partnership with NVIDIA to optimize AI workloads on Azure.
Microsoft’s Teams platform surpasses 400 million monthly active users.
The company unveils the Azure Maia AI accelerator to compete with Google and Amazon.
Microsoft’s Power BI platform gains traction in the enterprise analytics market.
The company launches the Surface Laptop 6, targeting business professionals.
The U.S. Department of Justice investigates Microsoft’s acquisition of Activision Blizzard.
The European Union fines Microsoft $1.2 billion for antitrust violations in cloud computing.
Microsoft settles a $20 million lawsuit over Xbox Live subscription practices.
The company faces a class-action lawsuit over Windows 11 update issues.
Microsoft complies with the EU Digital Markets Act, enabling third-party app stores on Windows.

China imposes restrictions on Microsoft’s cloud services over data security concerns.
India offers incentives for Microsoft to expand its data center footprint.

Satya Nadella sells $50 million worth of Microsoft shares, causing a temporary stock dip.
Microsoft hires a former Google executive to lead its AI division.
